author: EbonyAngel1
title: HP Printers—2nd to None!
content: Today I installed my new HP Envy Photo 7260r. It was almost too easy. HP has been my choice for the last 15 to 20 years, and there is no way I’d change. (I also have an HP Envy 5660 that just won’t quit. I’ll be donating it soon.) I’ve tried/used several other brands over the years, but none comes close to the ease of using an HP.  As stated setup is amazingly easy. Scanning is a breeze; the print quality is outstanding. Saving a document to a pdf from the print options is truly my favorite feature. Remote access is fantastic. author: JackMediaComments
title: Everyone Hates Printers, and it’s HP’s fault
content: Printers have always been hated, but they used to have more hardware issues than software. Every time my mom needs to print or scan something this software is the issue. Required sign in is obviously a dark practice to sell user data, and it ruins the efficiency of the workflow. Then after complying to that privacy invasion, I am told “printer not responding” “scanner unavailable” and the solution is to restart? Anyone involved in planning, coding, and/or promoting this app should go on strike, and then just quit, for the betterment of society. If you rated this app above 2 stars, please include how much they are paying you. I’m so relieved that my generation never uses scanners or printers, I am excited for this company to go bankrupt and die. Shout out to the movie Office Space, I hate everything about printers and it’s their fault.

author: Yes, the Eyes Have It
title: First time setup with HP 7000
content: It took only 20 minutes to do the initial setup for my new printer.  I am not a technical expert by any means.  I love things that work well and are user friendly.  Thanks HP!

author: PuddyPal
title: WTF
content: It shouldn’t be this hard! Brand new printer. Brand. New cartridges. Printing blank papers. Why can’t someone just talked to me. I’ve tried all the online fixes. Nothing works and it’s frankly pissing me off. Wasted time wasted money. You really should consider a phone number We can call free of charge. I’m taking the printer back now. I’ll never buy another HP computer.

author: Fun yet too expensive
title: One Stop Shop!
content: So far, so good. The app seems to function properly, and you can do almost anything—copy, ink, paper, order paper, printers, you name it, not to mention all these printing tools. They have a lot of options, you name it. Overall, I am impressed so far, though there’s a subscription involved. Other than that, give it a shot. What do you have to lose?
